channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: DOT price targets for this cycle? — 25 replies — 100.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
DOT price targets for this cycle?

Open thread • Replies: 25 • Updated: 2026-03-01 19:46 UTC

OP

DOT price targets for this cycle?

Early replies (first 3)

61916592
>>61916585
>WWE

These are your fellow investors.

61916663
$5 best case scenario

61916705
0

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61917099
>>61916663

I see 80 dollars

61917603
>>61916585
100.69$

61917609
>>61916585
Also consider using; https://app.interlay.io/lending or https://app.bifrost.io/farming?network=polkadot or https://app.hydration.net/strategies. You'll get more while also supporting the network with your activity. It's a double W

61917613
>>61916585
>this cycle
We are in a bear market. Below $1.

61917631
>>61916585
>>61917609
Another W is that you can INSTANTLY take your DOT back, whereas if you use an exchange to stake you're forced to wait the 28D (soon the dynamic unbonding will get implemented, but still. What is better? Instant unstaking or having to wait for a few days and not get any money while you wait?)

61917636
>>61916585

How do I stake

61917640
>>61917636
Read here >>61917609
Or you can also use; https://staking.polkadot.cloud/#/overview BUT you'll get less while also being subject to the unstaking queue... I don't recommend this method, BUT it's still better to use staking.polkadot.cloud than any of the CEXs

61917693
>>61916663
>5000% apy for staking
>$5 earned eoy
Seems very legit, my friend

61917701
>>61917693
HEEEELLLL YEAAAA BROOOOTHEEEERRRR WOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOO

Halving in 13 days https://polkadothalving.com/. The APY os going down to 5.6%. The inflation will be around 2.95624% because of that, because not all of DOT is staked. 52.79% is staked, therefore inflation = 0.5279 * 5.6% = 2.95624%. Here https://staking.polkadot.cloud/#/overview you can CTRL+F "Supply staked".

61919244
75 or 37

61919447
>>61916585

500

61919452
>>61916585
That's a funny way of spelling DOGE

61919561
>>61916585
25
>>61916592
Worked into a shoot
>>61919452
Unlimited mining isn't a good model

61919714
>>61916585

61919750
>>61919561
You won't be saying that when there's no more BTC to mine and your txs stop going through because there's no one to validate because the tx fees aren't enough to cover the cost of electricity to mine a block and then BTC has to fork and create more buttcoins to mine.
Every coin is unlimited.

Also, wanna know something funny?
There's roughly 1,999,999,900,000,000 satoshis in circulation.
And 168,900,000,000 doge.

61920407
>>61919750
>You won't be saying that when there's no more BTC to mine and your txs stop going through because there's no one to validate because the tx fees aren't enough to cover the cost of electricity to mine a block and then BTC has to fork and create more buttcoins to mine.
You literally don't understand how mining works, do you?

61920527
>>61920407
What exactly do you think happens when BTC halves?
What do you think will happen when the block reward turns to zero and the supply is fully realized?
Or did you not know that the block reward will eventually become zero because no new BTC are being printed?

61920591
>>61920527
>What exactly do you think happens when BTC halves?
>What do you think will happen when the block reward turns to zero and the supply is fully realized?
>Or did you not know that the block reward will eventually become zero because no new BTC are being printed?
You literally don't understand how mining works, do you?

61920605
>>61920591
Are you trying to imply that the halving doesn't reduce the block rewards by half every 210k blocks?
Do YOU not understand how mining works?

61920663
>>61920605
>Are you trying to imply that the halving doesn't reduce the block rewards by half every 210k blocks?
>Do YOU not understand how mining works?
You literally don't understand how mining works, do you?

You'll need to think about how stuff works a little more...

61920683
>>61920663

61920700
>>61920683
Your thinking is correct and flawed at the same time. You fail to see some stuff. Everything you say is correct, yet it misses a lot of stuff.

item.title: I've saved €2.800. Should I be a NEET at my uncle's house for the next couple of years? — 4 replies — 16.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I've saved €2.800. Should I be a NEET at my uncle's house for the next couple of years?

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-03-01 19:45 UTC

OP

I've saved €2.800. Should I be a NEET at my uncle's house for the next couple of years?

Early replies (first 3)

61920581
>>61920564
nigga I saved 80k and i feel broke

61920587
2.8k is literally nothing

61920680
>>61920564
Can you even pay a month's rent in a major European city with that amount?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61920694
>>61920680
it's not that expensive.
I rent an apartment for €1.200 and been NEETing my life away for 2 years now

item.title: How to long Israel? With Iran out of the way, it's going to become THE dominant regional power. Do they have an S&P500 I… — 14 replies — 56.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
How to long Israel? With Iran out of the way, it's going to become THE dominant regional power. Do they have an S&P500 I…

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-03-01 19:46 UTC

OP

How to long Israel? With Iran out of the way, it's going to become THE dominant regional power. Do they have an S&P500 I can put my money in?

Early replies (first 3)

61919946
just long america, they're basically the same entity

61919948
get your ass back to /pol/, or rather just kill yourself hasbara larper

61920040
>>61919943
I knew you are a jew.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61920050
>>61919943
Those borders look real pretty

61920359
>>61919943
Turkroaches are next!

61920383
>>61919943
just get a chest filled with ancient silver shekels. some jew will probably even buy them from you for collector value if israel moons

61920385
>>61919943
Remphan is a faggot, and also dead.

61920479
>>61919943
doubt the country will still exist in a few decades

61920492
>>61919943
EIS
IZRL if you want tech focus

61920499
>>61919943
Buy American media companies. Military industrial complex (your tax money pays for Israel's defence). Palantir. Any companies ran by pedos blackmailed by the kikes. Any company promoting, or profiting from mass uncontrolled migration/negrification.

61920504
>>61919946
>just long america, they're basically the same entity
This is also true.
What reason do you have to believe the capital of the Anglo-Zionist Jewish Empire of Satan - srael is going to outperform the military golem America?

61920610
>>61919943
What happens after they obtain those borders?

61920618
>>61920610
Peace in the Middle-East is finally achieved

61920699
>>61919943
>With Iran out of the way, it's going to become THE dominant regional power.
What about Turkey? In fact, once they get rid of one entity, another pops up. And public sentiment wrt Israel in the West is at an all time low.

I would honestly take the bearish bet on Israel.

item.title: WOAH What year is it where am I? /biz/?? when did they add an iz? — 77 replies — 47.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
WOAH What year is it where am I? /biz/?? when did they add an iz?

Open thread • Replies: 77 • Updated: 2026-03-01 18:09 UTC

OP

WOAH
What year is it where am I?
/biz/?? when did they add an iz?

Early replies (first 3)

61906377
>>61906370
Whatever..
Finance?
Ummm okay
Ill brought you a gift /b/iz/?

61906378
>>61906370
Thats epic wing guy. Idk why its called that, but he's pretty epic.

61906379
>>61906377
Well would you look at that dubs
were off to a great start

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61909183
>jpg
Fake oldfags, all of you

61909185
>>61906370
We now have AI that is smarter than 99% of humans.

61909212
>>61907301
New anxiety unlocked

61909219
>>61906449

61909264
>>61906449

61909521
Bretty good thread

61909548
More like this. Based.

61909664
>>61906370
What memecoin is this?

61910663
Postan in an oldfag bread

61912801
Best thread rn

61914222
>>61906370

>>61906370

>not gif
>not 400x400

Regardless, I appreciate the thread and the effort. Weirdly, I was thinking of EFG just the other day.

61914231
best content on the 4chinz for years

61914300
I have been using 4chan since 2008 basically every day

61915467
>>61914222

Wasnt his name Optimized Gif Dude

It was low bandwidth versions of popular memes.

61915797
>>61914300
Same but make it 2004 and few months of break once in a while

61917312
>>61906458
>>61907301
I would really love to go back to post dial up but pre smartphone internet world and just stay there, basically everything past that point has been a downgrade and the few things that have improved aren't worth it at all

61917331
The saddest thing is that as you age and age you realize that the new gens will say the same thing. Their real quality is what they know too.

61917585
>incorrect file names
Salvia officinalis in fields

61917849
>>61906370
>jpg

61918416
great thread. i miss /b/.

61918426
This was not spam.

61918486
>>61909212
Anxiety? What? There’s nothing scary about that photo

61918948
>>61906370
Good thread. It's been a while since OP wasn't a faggot

61918968
>>61918416

It’s a cesspit now. Dont even look. Man I’m 46! Where did the time go.

61920110
>>61918968
dang unc, go outside

61920123
Great thread, man.

61920179
>>61906449
>oopsie
winrar

61920392
>>61906370
efg go up. ascend into higher realms of meme existence

item.title: Hyperliquid >decentralized perp and spot exchange >trading silver, gold and crude oil on weekends during a war >genius l… — 22 replies — 36.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Hyperliquid >decentralized perp and spot exchange >trading silver, gold and crude oil on weekends during a war >genius l…

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-03-01 19:11 UTC

OP

Hyperliquid

>decentralized perp and spot exchange
>trading silver, gold and crude oil on weekends during a war
>genius level founders. Harvard, MIT and caltech.
>no vc funding from day 1
>31% geneis airdop tokens to users minting millionaires for early usrs.
>97% all platfrom fees spent and buy HYPE tokens from open market and sent to burned wallet.


Holding HYPE is comfy AF

Early replies (first 3)

61918612
>>61918597
Buy an ad.
CLEAN IT UP JANNIES

61918623
>>61918597
>Shilled by literally all of Twitter fags including scammers like Arthur
>Token not needed
Yeah, no.

61918628
>>61918623
why don't people say "stock not needed". its a crypto company and the coin is basically the stock.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61918695
>>61918623
The coin might not be needed but they use it to buy and burn 98% of the profits so number go up and it’s destined to sit in the top 5 by EOY

61918707
It probably has the best price action out there. And it's not listed on most major exchanges

>>61918695
Isn't hype used as gas for the hyperliquid chain?

61918832
>>61918623
hype is great. non kyc leverage trading??? plus it had a multitude of apps being built to take advantage of it from fully automated leverage trading apps like jest to shit like dreamcash. Claude being used on it too. its here to stay

61918837
>>61918832
More info about those apps? I thought it was only used for the hyperliquid exchange

61918843
>>61918597
Looks like asters butt plug.

61918925
>>61918597
shit volume on commodities
have fun getting adled
dot hl are insufferable fags

61919310
>>61918612
HYPEBTC has a nice bullflag setup

61919323
>>61918623
>token not needed

All 97% of all fees generated by the Hyperliquid exchange is used to buy HYPE tokens and gets sent to burn address.
$1.3 Billion is fees so far. All on chain.

0xfefefefefefefefefefefefefefefefefefefefe

61919354
>>61919323
Is the hype token used as gas for the chain?

61919376
Fuck hyperliquid, they forced cz the good guy to giganuke the market (like he did in 2022 to destroy ftx/sbf), but these fuckers are still alive.
They need to disappear so cz can allow the market to recover.

61919383
>>61919376
I don't hold any hype but I use the exchange on a daily basis and it works better than even centralized exchanges. It's the best product out there.

61919391
>>61919354
Yes. Hype is used as gas on the HyperEVM chain.

There are two chains in Hyperliquid ecosystem.

HyperCore - trading engine. No gas needed. Pay with trading fees which is used to buy and burn HYPE.

HyperEVM - smart-contract layer. HYPE used as bas.

61920112
I was thinking about grabbing a bag in late Jan and then it pumped again. Serves me right for wanting to buy this under 20 burgerbux

61920124
>>61920112
Same, bro. I just hold btc and trade on hyperliquid. I wanted to get myself a good back of hype, it's the best project out there and the team are not niggers. They want hype to go up. This weekends pump is because everybody is using it to trade crypto and commodities.

One of the few chains that is actually used. No marketing, no bullshit kantian philosophy or any kind of german idealism talk like Sergey or Charles hoskinson

61920169
How do I even buy Hype in the US? I need to use a VPN?

61920469
>>61919376

61920537
can i trade spot btc on there?

61920572
>>61920537
yes. Crypto, stocks, commodities... both spot and perps

61920574
Forex too

item.title: As an Eastern European, what economic impact will blockading the straight of Hormuz realistically have? — 29 replies — 21.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
As an Eastern European, what economic impact will blockading the straight of Hormuz realistically have?

Open thread • Replies: 29 • Updated: 2026-03-01 18:24 UTC

OP

As an Eastern European, what economic impact will blockading the straight of Hormuz realistically have? What effect will this have on my holdings of the US dollar in particular?

Early replies (first 3)

61919956
get your ass back to /pol/ you retarded hasbara larper. Or better, stream your suicide

61919964
>>61919949
Nothingburger.

61919965
>>61919956
I feel like legitimate financial discussions don't belong there.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61919966
no one cares about poor and irrelevant eastern europe
just move to germany and stfu

61919972
>>61919966
Oh, you think you're safe? Should I have written "as an American" instead? Or better yet nothing at all?

61919981
Its not 2003, this shit is mitigated+ priced in

61919984
>>61919965
get your ass back to /pol/ shill. Nobody gives two shits about the script you were contracted to propagate in this frame

61920001
Half this site is bots, and half of what's left are edgelord teenagers. "As an American", the majority of normalized peers are not playing some kind of "fuck everyone that's not us" anthem on loop 24/7, but you'd sure think so reading this kind of shit

61920006
>>61920001
based

61920010
>>61919949
If you don't already hold LMT or XLE then you already missed the opportunity to make money on this event.

61920030
>>61920001
>fuck everyone that's not us
yeah no, americans actually do think this way
that's why they elected Trump twice

61920037
>>61919949
Iran said they wont block it. Retarded media.

61920058
I think 20-25% global oil and gas comes through it. Plus the offshore rigs are in danger also.

61920062
Keep an eye on Suez. Egyptian population is boiling at the government currently. Of that closes it compounds things.

61920066
>>61920037
Why are you lying?
The IRGC has orders to block it retard

61920072
Reminder that Uncle Jeff specifically recommended shorting shipping futures under precisely conditions such as these.
But is even that priced in?
What if the whole thing was a masterminded plot to snag liquidity over this exact scenario!
Oh the madness never ends.

61920149
>>61919949
Dollar will be fine, US economy will be fine, countries that import crude oil are loosers (China, Europe). It depends a lot on how long this conflict will be.

61920157
>>61920149
CPI is at 3.5 because of low energy costs. Inflation is about to get out of control again. Rate hikes incoming , gonna need the ol volker raise to 20% to fix this

61920161
>>61919949
As an Eastern European most of your petroleum an gas comes from Russia, despite what the media larpers and politicians will tell you. In practical terms this has no impact on your life. In realistic terms the corrupt politicians will conspire with oligarchs to raise food and gas prices and any other products they can get away with and to calm the masses point to Iran and say
>But look what's happening on the other side of the world it's not our fault just look! We must support Israel and pass more anti-semitism laws for cheaper fuel.

61920189
>>61919949
>what economic impact will blockading the straight of Hormuz realistically have?
We will see on market open what happens; and more importantly, over the coming weeks, what happens to the price of oil. Right now, on weekend trading CFDs (very low liquidity, not a lot of accuracy to the real open, but still merit in looking at how they are trading) oil is up more than 9% - if this were to carry over to futures open at 6:00pm EST tonight, would be $73 WTI. This is not a high enough price to cause a crisis outright, but, if the price continues to trend higher, especially if oil ever goes above $80 a barrel, we unironically may see a massive global economic crisis. This would occur because bonds worldwide have been priced/trading assuming there will be a cut in interest rates due to economic weakness. However.. if the price of oil was to sharply rise and continue to rise, this will completely destroy the ability for central banks to cut interest rates, as the price of oil is (arguably) the single most important input to inflation due to its use in 10,000 products/byproducts & how it is used for transportation of goods and services. Banks & credit institutions are already extremely underwater on debt holdings -- if inflation surged higher (and right now, inflation is already trending higher: https://tradingeconomics.com/united-states/pce-price-index-monthly-change) it will force a violent repricing in federal funds futures / rate cut expectations / a sharp rise in interest rates (meaning a sharp decrease in bond prices); literally may cause hundreds of bank failures world wide
I want to emphasize that this is the worst case scenario to come from this, where it's a lot more likely that the price of oil stabilizes in the $65-80 range and then demand decreases from economic output have it downtrend in price again - crisis averted, nothing burger
80% chance nothing burger
20% chance financial crisis

61920205
>>61919949
>>61920189
>What effect will this have on my holdings of the US dollar in particular?
It depends if the US dollar is still seen as safehaven currency in a crisis like this. In general, the dollar has been seen as the least dirty shirt in the laundry basket
However.. it seems in the last year or two that this narrative is buckling -- hence the rapid rise in the price of gold & silver [alternative currencies to the dollar]
Historically, the dollar would do well in a prolonged conflict scenario, investors worldwide flight to dollar in times of geopolitical uncertainty
Theoretically as well, if the price of oil were to sharply rise and continue to rise, buyers of oil worldwide would need more dollars to purchase the same amount of oil, demand for dollar rises - dollar remains strong
In addition, if there was fear of inflation returning, forcing higher interest rates, the higher that US bonds yield, the greater the demand to buy US bonds as the bonds are more attractive as an investment; the bonds can only be purchased in dollars, so demand for dollars also rises
However, every crisis is different - history doesn't repeat itself, but it rhymes; so you never know if this time may be different. If issues of US solvency become evident, then the dollar will completely and utterly shit the bed
80% chance dollar rises in value relative to other currencies
20% chance dollar shits the bed

61920280
>>61920030
If you think the outspoken earth-is-flat MAGA cases or the screaming purple haired leftoids are the average American, you're far off the mark. Believe it or not, most of the smart ones see all the noise and speak softly with one-another while the retarded kid in keeps huffing glue and screaming in the classroom. And the average american isn't pleased with either side of our politics, mainly because corporations are running our governmental policies through corrupted systems, instead of the government keeping corporations honest, as it was intended. The Internet isn't accurately representative of the real world, kiddo, and the powers that be prefer you to hate any fucking group other than the man behind the curtain. So keep finding pencil thin bridges of reason to hate your fellow human over sex, skin color, religion, sexual preference, home country, hell, hate the police and the soldiers, as long as you keep hating each-other and devolve into endless bickering over who's getting fucked harder so you don't collectively start looking at the commonality systems in place that oppress all of you, like the brainless sow that you are.

61920286
>>61920001
Low test post

61920291
>>61920161
This.
There's not an unbiased news source in the world, and the best you can find are aggregation sites that try to balance polarising views. You're like sheep to slaughter, following the brainless sheep in front of you without a thought, and I am here for it.

61920296
>>61920189
>We will see on market open what happens
Depending on additional news tonight, I was already in my gut feeling the mood of exchanging some of my dollars after a slight recovery against my currency this month. But it could be nothing more than the usual speculation mood-swing based panic if there is a sell off.

61920304
>>61920286
>>61920280

> Self-reporting example offers itself for submission

61920408
>>61920304
>le enlightened centrist

Is it difficult breathing up there on the peak of the bell curve?

61920424
>>61919949
Buy oil tanker stocks

61920432
>>61920408
> Low test post
> Le enlightened centrist

Yes, it is. Do carry on, you absolute unit.

item.title: Was telling some mechanic wfh engineer how he has no life because he has a job. — 35 replies — 18.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Was telling some mechanic wfh engineer how he has no life because he has a job.

Open thread • Replies: 35 • Updated: 2026-03-01 17:53 UTC

OP

Was telling some mechanic wfh engineer how he has no life because he has a job. He replies with
>if I had no life, I would kms
I go
>you spend most of your time at work. 40 hours a week and 5 out of 7 days. Work IS your life.
He goes
>no, I sleep 8 hours, work 7.5 hours and chill 8 hours.

Are normies this retarded?

Early replies (first 3)

61918491
he's out there living life right now on a Saturday night while you're here posting

61918517
>>61918491
I don’t need to do anything on any specific day like a working class person.

61918538
By default, because he doesn't post on this site, that makes him more free and an overall better person than you.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61918596
>>61918586
When home prices drop across the board, people don’t have money to buy.

61918605
>>61918478
To qualify for a mortgage on a median priced home today, you need roughly $127,000 in household income. The median household makes about $80,000. Nearly 75% of homes on the market are UNAFFORDABLE for the average American family. Three out of four.

61918607
>>61918596
skill issue

61918637
>>61918478
You sound like a pathetic retarded faggot trying to justify his meaningless life.

61918676
>>61918607
No it isn’t. Prices drop but people lose their jobs.

61918684
Why are you here if you are wealthy?

61918702
>>61918684
Epstein posted here bro

61918710
>>61918605
Average home price in my city is 900k.
And isn’t the median household income 60k?

61918874
>>61918605
>>61918605
They wouldn't give me a loan because I don't make enough money, so I just bought my $525,000 house outright with my savings. Fuck those bank cocksuckers. Now I just have to pay property taxes.

61920119
>>61918676
If you haven't saved up since 2008 waiting for the REAL housing crash, you are simply NOT going to make it.

as said, skill issue.

61920132
Your ancestors worked twelve hour days, six days a week doing subsistence labor for potatoes, eating meat only during major holidays but you think working eight hours a day in an air-conditioned office and purchasing an international vacation with your labor hours is some kind of slavery because you watched Fight Club. A lot of you are on the border of delusion with the nonsense you spout. You have no knowledge of the past, zero conceptualization of the future. But here you are, making half-assed posts on a horticulture enthusiast image forum.

61920148
>>61920132
Youre right the ancestors were kind of dumb

61920162
>>61920132
No they fucking didn’t.
Have you ever grown a potato in your life? You work hard a few days to get it all in the ground then you simply wait and chill until harvest. Fucking noble retard. Can’t even till.

61920163
>>61920119
wait wait wait, are you saying you gonna spend 18 years of saving up the liquidity from waging and risk taking to dump it onto a depreciating asset that has a naturally declining demand AND is going to get nuked because it has been used as a same asset re-leveraged ponzi collateral for 60 years?
AHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHA

61920166
>>61920119
>he "saves" up before making a purchase
ishiggidiggi

61920175
>>61920162
its really about time to cull the pseudo aristocracy of ponzi players of polity, bureaucracy, banker communists, subsidized "privates" and 50+ — they all deserve to die unnatural deaths

61920178
>>61920162
You’re legitimately dumb if you think our ancestors just sat around doing nothing. They worked their dicks off for shelter and food, with not much to spare.

61920183
>>61920178
>its history
>its culture
>do as I say
>please dont kill me
too late. You gonna die

61920187
>>61920178
Yeah, I'm with this guy. Subsistence farming fucking sucked, hence why thurdies would rather toil away in a mine inhaling sulfur fumes and dunking their nuts in mercury, rather than work on a field as a farmer

61920201
>>61920187
Its not that bad it just pays pretty bad. I think.

61920206
>>61920183
No idea what you’re talking about. You’re delusional if you think labor conditions and incentives for work haven’t exponentially gotten better over the past century.
>>61920187
People act like farming is some dope shit. Actually no, it sucks balls which is why we get immigrants to do it.

61920210
>>61920201
If you’re a laborer, yeah. If you’re a farmer with several hundred acres, you make an okay living. It’s a weird sector because the government can’t let you go out of business, so you’ll see white trash in a brand new truck they bought with farm bill money.

61920218
>>61920210
Many farmers went out of business recently lol

61920226
>>61920119
You are retarded.

61920228
>>61918478
lmao, I barely do any work and bought a second house on a lake last year, how poor are you all that you can't even afford one house?

61920234
>>61920218
This usually happens when the third or fourth generation realizes it’s a terrible job. Nobody is taking over for a relative, it just sucks. So they’ll sell the land for several million to some real estate developer probably.

61920258
>>61920163
>>61920166
>>61920226
real estate kikes are scared shitless

61920261
>>61920206
>back in my days
Gramps, your life is over. Your perception of reality is as relevant as a wet fart from last week. Honest suggestion, instead of trying to shill your ideals, kill yourself

61920279
>>61920258
they certainly are. The only reason the akiya market hasnt yet corrected to 1920 levels is because the greatest fools: funds, banks, asset management companies and other ponzi players have acquired the boomer stock that was thrown on the market over the last 4 years with the sole purpose to not see the collateral value of their re-leveraged ponzi schemes melt away and leave them as their creditors bankrupt, holding worthless concrete wood boomer boxes

61920336
>>61920132
expect they were drunk the whole time or on drugs.
and those Drugs and booze used to be cheap!
now you need to be a highly functionally addict with a full time job to afford the habits

61919749
>>61919704

61920487

item.title: Identity stolen — 9 replies — 9.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Identity stolen

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-03-01 18:49 UTC

OP

I'm pretty sure i got my identity stolen. i moved to an apartment last may, and a month ago i got a letter in the mail from a nearby bank saying that I have "interest income" on an account with them. its only $14.31, but they have my SSN on the letter, along with my name and the apartment address. my legal billing address is still at my moms house a county away.

what do i do? my mom said i could take my SS Card and birth certificate to the bank and withdraw all the money, but fucking over someone who already stole my identity seems like a bad idea.

Early replies (first 3)

61918487
>>61918013
Contact the bank and say that that's not your account and that you believe you've had your identity stolen. They should be able to point you in the right direction.

61918503
Worst they can do is ruin your credit score. Fuckem lol

61918525
>>61918013
deposit $0.57

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61919816
>>61918525
the $14.31 is the interest on the income from the account, not the money in the account itself

61919840
Ring the bank and ask for more details because you received letter about account you don't recognise. It could be a forgotten account rather than stolen identity. Maybe some utilities thing set up by a landlord or something.
Seems very unlikely someone is going to come fuck you up over money in an account in your name. If it is legit stolen identity, your bank can point you in right direction

61919888
>>61918013
Your mom is an idiot, contact the bank and let them know you don’t recognize the account. Ask when it was created and if it was done online or in person. Someone could have stolen your ID, opened an account, and is laundering money using your name.

61920003
>>61918013
Ask ur bank not 4chan lol

61920496
>>61919840
i checked with my landlord incase they invested my security check or something but they said no.

>>61919888
i haven't lost any ID's recently, i really only have my drivers license. but yeah seems like i need to call the bank and sort it out.

>>61920003
i was hoping you guys would tell me how to take the money out and then lock down my identity

61920514
>>61918013
>>61918487
Literally walk into the bank and tell them you don't have an account with them.

item.title: Rolling thread. I got 15k in BTC. 5k in XRP. 7k in BNB. 4K in DOGE. and 3k in PEPE. (Cuz the short time movement makes m… — 34 replies — 9.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Rolling thread. I got 15k in BTC. 5k in XRP. 7k in BNB. 4K in DOGE. and 3k in PEPE. (Cuz the short time movement makes m…

Open thread • Replies: 34 • Updated: 2026-03-01 16:02 UTC

OP

Rolling thread.
I got 15k in BTC.
5k in XRP.
7k in BNB.
4K in DOGE.
and
3k in PEPE. (Cuz the short time movement makes my pepe go hard)
Dubs decide what I drop my 1k gambling money* on.
*Coin must be on Binance and the numbers given are in my local currency.

Early replies (first 3)

61914191
chainlink

61914195
>>61914184
Long XAGUSDT perp futures contracts. 50x leverage.

61914201
>>61914191
Thank god you failed. I dont want to be honor bound to lose money.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61914523
>>61914184
Another 1k into XRP.
alternatively, Eth or Solana.

61914529
>>61914184

duinocoin

61914533
bingus token

61914563
>>61914533
We have a winner. Stand by.

61914582
>>61914533
Im sorry friend, but I cant find a coin named BINGUS on Binance spot trade. Therefore I am forced to call your dubs null and void. Unless I am simply to stupid to see it.

61914601
just buy @epsteinbitcoin or @jeevacation12 username on fragment and resell for profit noob

61914609
>>61914523
I would like to add a bit to my XRP investment, but that is too long term to make this fun. So I am glad you didnt score the dubs, if you will excuse me flippant-cye.

61914612
Can you donate some money to me? I'm in massive debt... I'll make an interesting video in return!

61914617
>>61914601
I tried to invest in COCKSUCKER token but I didnt want to compete with a major whale like yourself.

61914621
>>61914612
The Check is in the mail.

61914623
>>61914621
Please... I really am in a tough spot, if genuine help is possible please contact me at: sketchycornerofficial@gmail.com

61914642
>>61914623
You got mail.

61914708
>>61914642
I didn't...

61914722
>>61914708
Check the business folder.

61914739
what a homosexual thread

61914749
Dump it all on VVV, the private ai token biz missed 5x in 3 months

61914766
>>61914739
At least its not botosexual.

61914783
rolling for luck

61914821
>>61914783As far as I know there is no such coin. But lucky you did not score a *get* so I am spared trying to find one.

61914830
>>61914201
Retards like you are going to rope. 35k is barely enough as it is for 1 coin. And you have it spread out in an equal pie chart between 5 coins that have already mooned. So even if you get a 50x out of one of them (which won’t happen) you’ll still be below anons here currently in low 6 figure hell that want to kill themselves right now.

If you put that all into Link you’ll have a comfy 3k stack and will actually make it. Remember, Link hasn’t mooned. Most of the year and a half that it went from $1 to $50 it steadily rose. By the time it got to the top 5 the entire crypto community was like huh? It hasn’t even left the launch pad yet. It just slowly went up the ladder to get to the launch pad and then just been ranging down with most other shit since until it gigmoons.

61914846
>>61914830
Give me a pic related of Link, well and good zoomed out.

61915450
>>61914846
You are going to kys. Imagine having barely enough for 1 coin, having zero chance of making it with it being diversified, while actually being on a Link board where everyone knows, and still not sticking it all in link and getting 3k which will very likely be a mega stack to everyone in the world outside this board by 2030. It would be one thing if you like only kinda knew about link in passing and weren’t able to decipher that it will be the one. But you are actually on the cutting edge board for link and aren’t invested. Fucking doge? 1/4th of a BTC? You are going to be so fucked. At least put half of all that garbage you have into Link and get 1,500 coins. Fucking retard.

61915490
>>61915450
>Hoes mad

61915624
zerpee

61919007
XRP

61919861
>>61914184
rubic (stays cubic)

61920004
solana

61920013
>>61914184
Is giga on binance? It's so low but stuck it doesn't seem it's gonna go lower. I'm thinking of tossing in a few hundred as a meme bet.

61920016
>>61920004
It's prob this dubs or not

61920023
>>61914184
Liquidate all of that and go all in $34k on XRP. Bridge to Flare Network, then throw it in the various DeFi protocols/pools, then wait 10 years. Thank me later.

item.title: So Tradingview is over $400 US a month if you want to see 1 min charts? — 25 replies — 7.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
So Tradingview is over $400 US a month if you want to see 1 min charts?

Open thread • Replies: 25 • Updated: 2026-03-01 16:30 UTC

OP

So Tradingview is over $400 US a month if you want to see 1 min charts? I was told I need Ultimate, where can you see and trade 1 min charts for free or cheaper than that?

Early replies (first 3)

61918479
$461 worth of product but they are willing to give it to you for free for 1 month, thats a $461 giveaway.

61918533
>>61918479
Even free tradingview has minute charts, you have to go up to premium if you want seconds based intervals. I don't know where your getting that price from. I'm showing $240 for ultimate if you go with the most expensive billed monthly option. But if you seriously want tradingview, get whatever minimum plan you can stand until black friday, that's when they have their once a year huge sale on annual plans.

61918574
>>61918474
Trading view sucks so much it's unreal. No wonder topstep made their own trading platform.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61918578
>>61918474
imagine paying $400 a month to see 1m charts when you could just $400 long

61918579
>trading 1 min charts

utterly pointless lol

61918641
>>61918474
>>61918479
>>61918533
>>61918574
>>61918578
>Paying for something that is free

do retards really lmao

anything below 4hours is for retards anyway

61918644
>>61918474
>15 watchlist alerts
>ability to BUY professional market data

61918648
>>61918579
>>61918641
You stupid faggots realize that a lot of people make hundreds of thousands or millions of dollars a year trading nothing but 1 - 5 minute charts right?

61918660
>>61918648
no they dont

61918681
>>61918648
proof?

61918744
>>61918660
>>61918681
Lmao, sheltered retards... why are you on /biz? Fabio Valentini is considered one of the current best traders in the world and trades strictly on one and five minute charts. He is one among thousands... you guys are so fucking clueless it's embarrassing. Don't talk about trading ever again.

61918761
>>61918744
>saaar please pay $500 for trading view saaar need more indicators and 1 minute chart to be good trader

kek you fool

61918799
>>61918761
I get 1 minute amd any timeframe charts for free on my trading platform, don't need or use trading view nor do I give any fucks if somebody pays them or not. Did you want to address how wrong and uninformed you are about scalping and traders who make more in a year than you will in your entire life on one minute time frames or shift goal posts even further?

61918807
>>61918648
and how many don't?

61918845
>>61918474
Tradingjew kek

61918847
>>61918641
Pay up tards!

61918850
>>61918744
I have seen many cex and another jew corpo shill on biz, but tv kek, we have reached rock bottom, iq wise.

61918997
>>61918474
Binance?

61919653
>>61918648
I trade the 1 nanosecond chart

61919657
>>61918474
you need pro to see 1 second charts, which costs 60/mo, but if you buy on holdays is only like 216/yr instead of 720. the ultimate tier is to see tick charts, which are very cool, but certainly not worth it for someone who makes this thread.

61919663
>>61918474
1-minute is available for free on my end just fine, just checked. I see the 2-minute, 10-minute, and sub-1-minute charts are all locked behind a subscription, which is the same case as it's always been.

61919664
>>61918474
What's the point of that? You get 1m charts for free, and 1m charts are kind of useless.

61919789
>>61919657
My broker shows the tape but it comes in blocks, not sure if it's because my computer/their javashit software sucks or if it's just the data limitation.
I got lucky scalping a few times but it always felt like luck, and if you're too slow you're now stuck "swing trading" a quickly losing position.

61919871
>>61919789
what platform? regardless check your quote speed, you may have different options like real time, 1 second delay and so on

61920118
>>61918648
yes sir we have the realize sar

61915005

61915010
>>61914991
i have a certified iq of 143 and i'm telling you the markets will open higher on monday

61915015
>>61914991

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61916148
>>61916079

61916155
So is this time the real deal? Not like last year when it was just some tactical bombings?

61916159
Do people not realize that Iran mines a fuck ton of crypto which means they will have to start a fuck ton to fund this war?

61916176
>>61916118
You have no clue what you're talking about. The leadership is a bunch of Arabs thats killing Persians aka real Iranians.

61916178
>>61916176

I fucking hate Iranians who still call themselves that

61916201
>>61915858
>>61916079
Lol what is this 60 year old guy typing dawg that aint it

61916203
Republicans starting wars, again

61916208
I unironically have TDS; this orange baboon drives me fucking crazy. Get me out of this timeline.

61916211
Iran is now bombing crypto influencers in Dubai lmao

61916240
>>61916211
the fuck? source or didn't happen

61916251
I will be honest, I hope a lot of burgers will die for Israel. You burgers deserve much more for subversion you suffered and did nothing about it. Now your military is completely controlled by the tribe and your carriers and planes are doing what Bibi tells them to do. Embarrassing.

61916254
>>61916240
Just look up Iran missile Dubai

61916479
Geopolitical bros, what are your thoughts about this? Do you think this will end up like what happened a year ago or this is the real deal?

I wouldn't like to see a v shape recovery.

Pls, I don't wanna have to go to /pol/ and talk to those people.

61916505
>>61916479
Unpopular opinion: Nothing ever happens.
The US does not have the industry capacity for a war with Iran.

61916522
>>61915128
He’s ending the war, not starting it, dipshit.

61916526
>>61916251
What do you think will happen, fag, when there is no one left to fight in the Middle East?

Cannibalize the master...

61916528
>>61916522
We’ve always been at war with Eurasia, etc etc.

61916704
>>61915888
For me it’s Metallica Mob

61916728
>>61916211
A selfless and victimless act.
My heartfelt commiseration goes to Iran.

61916733
>>61916479
Iran's regime cucked itself into a corner. It's being attacked by Israel and it's golem, all the while its populace hates the regime and want to overthrow it. It's literally ogre for the regime. All they can do now is go out with a bang and try to cause as much destruction as possible. The regime leadership should've fled to Russia when they had the chance.

61916849
Do you guys think the mexican peso will be affected much if at all by this?
I was shorting EURMXN friday. i think i might be earning a ton when the market opens

61918083
>>61915858
>the baby-slurpers believe in some kind of color-blind utopia

61918090
>>61915027

61918142
>>61916733
who the fuck would care what an average normgroid in some country thinks
its 2026, if you can't manufacture consent, you have no business ruling anyone

61918181
>>61914991
Biggest nothingburger of this year already

61918233
>>61915655
>Muslims are a bigger threat than Jews
Said no non-jew ever. At least try to blend in if you're going to make gey propaganda.

61918353
>>61918181
yep. the US could literally nuke several countries at once and there would be no consequences whatsoever, just some stupid drama for a few days. this is the uncomfortable truth the rest of the world doesn't like to hear.

61918560
>>61915033
165 here and I don't even care. May as well let them kill each other half way across the planet.

61918785
I unironically think we need more war so that markets stop overreacting to it. Just let my ETFs steadily go up in peace.

item.title: INFLATION IS GOING TO GET CRAZY!!!!! — 12 replies — 5.0/hr

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-03-01 17:22 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

61919027
You do realize when Covid started the first thing the retards went to stockpile was toilet paper. You’ll be able to trade your worthless cash for anything. It’ll be a lot easier for you to survive than people trying to barter with Merc dimes and liberty halves.

61920082
>>61919023
>THERE IS NOTHING LEFT CHEAP TO BUY
that might change soon if this situation is not a nothing burger

>>61919023

61920114
>>61920104

>suggests the pumpingest asset that ever pumped
good job proving his point

61920122
>>61920114
Pe 22, peg less then one , compared to mu and Sandisk it is basement bargain .
I am going to make it , how about you anon?

61920133
…why? Gasoline volatility has big short term inflationary pressure but pretty minimal longterm effects.

61920136
>>61920133
oil spikes cause recessions all the time retard
see '08

61920144
>>61920136
Damn dude if you think the 08 recession was caused by oil spikes, I don’t know what to tell you.

Inflation =/= recession btw

61920146
>>61919023

61920155
You’re better off holding anything than the dollar. It’s just so fucking worthless now. It’s the new peso.

>went out with wife the other day for dinner
>two plates of pasta, sparkling water and tiramisu was $100
Like what the fuck is this?

61920176
>>61920155

61920250
>>61919023
If nothing is cheap, crazy inflation is already here
Oil is still cheap, and if it dips on Monday because Iran is seen as a nothingburger, I'm buying
